title: [Bug]: Bug-report sync accepts duplicate JSON keys
summary: The managed bug-report sync tool uses default JSON parsing and can silently accept duplicate keys when preparing public GitHub bug issues.
observed: A bug report JSON document with duplicate observed or title fields can be loaded with only the final value retained.
expected: Duplicate keys in bug-report JSON should be rejected before a public issue body or lifecycle comment is generated.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that writes a bug report with a duplicate title key and calls load_bug_report.
impact: Bug reports are public audit records for test-driven fixes. Ambiguous local JSON can make the evidence trail harder to trust.
delivery_semantics: No runtime delivery behavior changes. This fix hardens bug-management automation only.
security: The fix must retain existing public-issue leak prevention and must not publish private locators, credentials, or payload details.
acceptance: A focused regression test proves duplicate bug-report JSON keys are rejected.
Valid bug reports continue to sync.
The error message is clear for maintainers.
Existing bug sync tests and full checks still pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, bug-report sync tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md and bug workflow documentation if public behavior changes.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published. Include the release tag, regression evidence, verification evidence, and any remaining limitations.
